zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Wake於1321年出生在英國薩默塞特度斯東時，她的父親John Wake39歲，母親Alice Blisworth22歲。她於大約1327年與John Keynes在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1359年享年38歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特多利申 韋克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Wake于1321年出生在英国薩默塞特度斯東时，她的父亲John Wake39岁，母亲Alice Blisworth22岁。她于大约1327年与John Keynes在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1359年享年38岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特多利申 韋克。

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: occupational name for ‘(man of) the watch’, from Middle English wak, wake ‘wake, vigil, watch, period of guard duty’ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil ‘(one who is) watchful, alert’. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille ‘of the watch, watchman’, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phàil ‘son of Paul’.

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Wake was born in 1321, in Durston, Somerset, England, her father, John Wake, was 39 and her mother, Alice Blisworth, was 22. She married John Keynes about 1327, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1359, at the age of 38, and was buried in Dowlish Wake, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Wake est née en 1321, à Durston, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John Wake, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice Blisworth, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé John Keynes environ 1327, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1359, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Dowlish Wake, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Wake 1321 in Durston, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Wake 39 und ihre Mutter Alice Blisworth 22. Sie hat um 1327 in England John Keynes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1359 im Alter von 38 Jahren und wurde in Dowlish Wake, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Wake è nata il nel 1321 nel Durston, Somerset, England, sua padre, John Wake, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Alice Blisworth, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato John Keynes circa 1327, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1359, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Dowlish Wake, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Wakeが1321年にイギリスソメルセトダルストンで生まれた時，父John Wakeは39歳，母Alice Blisworthは22歳であった。約1327年にイギリスでJohn Keynesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1359年に38歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトドウリッシュ ワクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Wake(이)가 1321년 영국 서머세트 뒤르스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Wake(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Blisworth(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1327년 영국에서 John Keynes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1359년 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 다우리시 웨이크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Wake nasceu em 1321, em Durston, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Wake, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice Blisworth, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Keynes aproximadamente 1327, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1359, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Dowlish Wake, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Wake родилась в 1321 г. в местности дурстон, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John Wake было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Blisworth, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Keynes около 1327 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1359 г. в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности доулиш веик, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Wake nació en 1321, en Durston, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John Wake, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice Blisworth, tenía 22 años. Se casó con John Keynes aproximadamente 1327, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1359, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Dowlish Wake, Somerset, Inglaterra.
